name: World 3: Frozen Tundra

criteria.description: Tundra tile palette is visually distinct from Forest and Desert: solid tiles use ice-blue/white colors from COLORS.tundra (iceBlue '#A8D4E6', snowWhite '#E8F0F8', deepIce '#6B9CB8'). Tiles are drawn with Canvas primitives appropriate to a frozen environment (ice surfaces, snow-covered platforms).
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1 (Frozen Lake)
Take screenshot of gameplay area
Verify tiles use tundra palette colors (ice-blue/white tones), not forest greens or desert sands

page.evaluate(() => { const colors = typeof COLORS !== 'undefined' ? COLORS.tundra : null; return colors && colors.iceBlue === '#A8D4E6' && colors.deepIce === '#6B9CB8' && colors.snowWhite === '#E8F0F8'; })
Verify tundra palette constants exist and Renderer uses them for World 3 rendering
criteria.threshold: Tundra tiles visually distinct from forest and desert, using tundra palette colors

criteria.description: Tundra parallax background with at least 3 layers renders behind all World 3 stages. Layers feature tundra-appropriate scenery (snow-capped mountains, frozen terrain, ice formations). An aurora borealis effect is visible in the sky — animated color bands or glowing curtains in green/teal tones.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1

page.evaluate(() => { if (typeof Camera !== 'undefined' && Camera.parallaxLayers) return Camera.parallaxLayers.length; if (typeof Camera !== 'undefined' && Camera.layers) return Camera.layers.length; const debug = window.__gameDebug; if (debug && debug.parallaxLayerCount) return debug.parallaxLayerCount(); return -1; })
Verify result >= 3
Take screenshot to verify tundra-themed background (ice-blue/white tones, aurora colors visible, not green forest or sand desert)
criteria.threshold: 3+ parallax layers with tundra theme and aurora borealis effect in World 3 stages

criteria.description: Falling snow particle effect is present in all tundra stages. Snow particles drift downward across the screen, creating a winter atmosphere. Particles are white/light-colored and continuously animate.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1 (Frozen Lake)
Take 2 screenshots ~30 frames apart
Verify visual evidence of falling snow particles (small white dots or flakes drifting downward)
Snow particles should be at different positions between the two screenshots (animated)
criteria.threshold: Visible falling snow particles in tundra stages that animate over time

criteria.description: Ice tile type exists (new tile ID or zone marker). When the player walks on ice surfaces, reduced friction causes sliding — the player takes longer to stop and to change direction. Ice physics apply in tundra stages, particularly stage 3-1 (Frozen Lake).
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1 (Frozen Lake)

page.evaluate(() => { const hasIceTile = typeof TILE_ICE !== 'undefined' || typeof TILES.ICE !== 'undefined'; const hasIceZones = typeof Level.iceZones !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.iceTiles !== 'undefined'; let iceCount = 0; if (typeof TILE_ICE !== 'undefined') { for (let y = 0; y < Level.height; y++) for (let x = 0; x < Level.width; x++) if (Level.getTile(x, y) === TILE_ICE) iceCount++; } return { hasIceTile, hasIceZones, iceCount }; })
Verify ice tiles or ice zones exist in stage 3-1 (count > 0 or zones defined)

page.evaluate(() => { const iceProps = typeof Player.onIce !== 'undefined' || typeof Player.icePhysics !== 'undefined' || typeof Physics.iceFriction !== 'undefined'; return { hasIcePhysics: iceProps }; })
Verify player has ice physics handling (reduced friction when on ice)
criteria.threshold: Ice tiles/zones exist in stage 3-1; player experiences reduced friction on ice surfaces

criteria.description: Ice block sliding puzzle mechanic exists in stage 3-2 (Crystal Caverns). Pushable ice blocks can be pushed along frictionless surfaces. Ice blocks slide until they hit a wall or obstacle. At least one ice block puzzle section exists in stage 3-2.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-2 (Crystal Caverns)

page.evaluate(() => { const hasIceBlocks = typeof Level.iceBlocks !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.pushableBlocks !== 'undefined'; let blockCount = 0; if (typeof Level.iceBlocks !== 'undefined') blockCount = Level.iceBlocks.length; else if (typeof Level.pushableBlocks !== 'undefined') blockCount = Level.pushableBlocks.filter(b => b.type === 'ice' || b.type === 'ice_block').length; return { hasIceBlocks, blockCount }; })
Verify ice blocks exist (count > 0)
Verify ice blocks have position and can slide (slide mechanic implemented)
criteria.threshold: Ice block sliding puzzle elements exist in stage 3-2 with push-to-slide behavior

criteria.description: Frost Imp enemy exists in tundra stages. Frost Imp attacks by throwing snowball projectiles in an arc trajectory. Snowballs that hit platforms freeze them (make them icy/slippery). Has at least 1 HP. Visually drawn as a small ice/frost creature using Canvas primitives with tundra palette colors.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1 (Frozen Lake)

page.evaluate(() => { const imps = Enemies.enemies.filter(e => e.type === 'frost_imp' || e.type === 'frostimp'); return imps.length > 0 ? { count: imps.length, health: imps[0].health, behavior: imps[0].behavior } : null; })
Verify at least 1 Frost Imp exists with health >= 1

page.evaluate(() => { const imp = Enemies.enemies.find(e => e.type === 'frost_imp' || e.type === 'frostimp'); if (!imp) return null; return { hasProjectile: typeof imp.projectileType !== 'undefined' || imp.behavior === 'projectile' || imp.behavior === 'snowball' || typeof imp.shootTimer !== 'undefined' }; })
Verify Frost Imp has projectile/snowball attack capability

page.evaluate(() => { const imp = Enemies.enemies.find(e => e.type === 'frost_imp' || e.type === 'frostimp'); if (!imp) return null; return { freezesPlatforms: typeof imp.freezesPlatforms !== 'undefined' ? imp.freezesPlatforms : null, hasFreezeAbility: typeof imp.freezeOnHit !== 'undefined' || typeof imp.snowballFreeze !== 'undefined' || (imp.projectileType && (imp.projectileType === 'freeze_snowball' || imp.projectileType === 'snowball')) }; })
Verify Frost Imp has platform-freezing capability (freezesPlatforms flag, freeze projectile type, or freeze-on-hit behavior)
criteria.threshold: Frost Imp present in tundra stages, has health >= 1, throws snowball projectiles that can freeze platforms

criteria.description: Ice Golem enemy exists in tundra stages. Ice Golem slides on ice surfaces (moves with ice friction physics) and shatters into pieces on death (death animation shows fragmentation). Has at least 2 HP (tougher than basic enemies).
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1 or 3-2

page.evaluate(() => { const golems = Enemies.enemies.filter(e => e.type === 'ice_golem' || e.type === 'icegolem'); return golems.length > 0 ? { count: golems.length, health: golems[0].health, behavior: golems[0].behavior } : null; })
Verify at least 1 Ice Golem exists with health >= 2

page.evaluate(() => { const golem = Enemies.enemies.find(e => e.type === 'ice_golem' || e.type === 'icegolem'); if (!golem) return null; golem.health = 0; golem.state = 'dying'; if (typeof golem.die === 'function') golem.die(); for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (typeof Enemies.update === 'function') Enemies.update(1/60); } return { state: golem.state, hasFragments: typeof golem.fragments !== 'undefined' && golem.fragments !== null, shattersOnDeath: golem.shattersOnDeath === true || golem.deathType === 'shatter' || golem.state === 'shatter' || golem.state === 'dying' }; })
Verify Ice Golem enters shatter/dying state on death (shattersOnDeath flag, fragments array exists, or shatter death state)
criteria.threshold: Ice Golem present in tundra stages, health >= 2, slides on ice, shatters on death

criteria.description: Snow Owl enemy exists in tundra stages. Snow Owl flies in a figure-8 or looping flight pattern and swoops toward the player. Cannot be reached easily from ground level. Visually drawn as a bird/owl using Canvas primitives.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-2 or 3-3

page.evaluate(() => { const owls = Enemies.enemies.filter(e => e.type === 'snow_owl' || e.type === 'snowowl'); return owls.length > 0 ? { count: owls.length, health: owls[0].health, behavior: owls[0].behavior, hasFlightPattern: typeof owls[0].flightTimer !== 'undefined' || typeof owls[0].swoopTimer !== 'undefined' || owls[0].behavior === 'figure8' || owls[0].behavior === 'flight' } : null; })
Verify at least 1 Snow Owl exists with health >= 1
Verify Snow Owl has flight pattern behavior (figure-8 or looping) and swoop mechanic
criteria.threshold: Snow Owl present in tundra stages, has flight pattern (figure-8 or loop), swoops at player

criteria.description: Stage 3-3 (Avalanche Peak) features auto-scrolling camera. The camera moves automatically to the right (or in a set direction), forcing the player to keep up. If the player falls behind the left edge of the scrolling camera, they take damage or die.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-3 (Avalanche Peak)

page.evaluate(() => { return { autoScroll: typeof Level.autoScroll !== 'undefined' ? Level.autoScroll : null, autoScrollSpeed: typeof Level.autoScrollSpeed !== 'undefined' ? Level.autoScrollSpeed : null, cameraAutoScroll: typeof Camera.autoScroll !== 'undefined' ? Camera.autoScroll : null, cameraAutoScrollSpeed: typeof Camera.autoScrollSpeed !== 'undefined' ? Camera.autoScrollSpeed : null }; })
Verify auto-scroll is enabled for stage 3-3 (autoScroll === true or autoScrollSpeed > 0)
Wait 60 frames, verify camera x position has increased automatically (camera scrolls without player input)

page.evaluate(() => { const camX = Camera.x; Player.x = camX - 100; Player.y = Player.y; const hpBefore = Player.health; for (let i = 0; i < 30; i++) { if (typeof Game.update === 'function') Game.update(1/60); else if (typeof Physics.update === 'function') Physics.update(1/60); } return { hpBefore, hpAfter: Player.health, playerState: Player.state, isDead: Player.state === 'dead' || Player.health <= 0 }; })
Verify player takes damage or dies when positioned behind the left edge of auto-scrolling camera (hpAfter < hpBefore or isDead === true)
criteria.threshold: Auto-scrolling camera active in stage 3-3; camera advances automatically; player takes damage or dies if left behind

criteria.description: Stage 3-3 has path fork sections during auto-scroll where the player must choose upper or lower routes. Both paths are traversable. This creates meaningful choice during the auto-scroll section.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-3 (Avalanche Peak)

page.evaluate(() => { if (typeof Level.forkSections !== 'undefined' && Level.forkSections.length > 0) return { method: 'forkSections', count: Level.forkSections.length }; if (typeof Level.pathForks !== 'undefined' && Level.pathForks.length > 0) return { method: 'pathForks', count: Level.pathForks.length }; let forkCount = 0; for (let x = 0; x < Level.width; x++) { let hasUpper = false, hasLower = false; const midY = Math.floor(Level.height / 2); for (let y = 1; y < midY - 1; y++) { const t = Level.getTile(x, y); if (t === 1 || t === 2 || (typeof TILE_ICE !== 'undefined' && t === TILE_ICE)) hasUpper = true; } for (let y = midY + 1; y < Level.height - 1; y++) { const t = Level.getTile(x, y); if (t === 1 || t === 2 || (typeof TILE_ICE !== 'undefined' && t === TILE_ICE)) hasLower = true; } if (hasUpper && hasLower) forkCount++; } return { method: 'tileAnalysis', forkColumns: forkCount, levelWidth: Level.width }; })
Verify path fork sections exist (forkSections/pathForks array with count > 0, or multiple columns with upper and lower platforms)
Take screenshot showing split-path area with platforms at different heights
criteria.threshold: Stage 3-3 has path fork sections with upper and lower routes during auto-scroll

criteria.description: Stage 3-1 (Frozen Lake) has a playable level layout at least 115 tiles wide with ice physics sections integrated into the terrain. Has defined spawn point, exit point, and boss arena. Contains Frost Imp and/or Ice Golem enemies.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, select stage 3-1, enter it

page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, height: Level.height, spawnX: Level.spawnX, spawnY: Level.spawnY, hasExit: typeof Level.exitX === 'number', hasBoss: Level.bossData !== null && Level.bossData !== undefined, enemyCount: Enemies.enemies.length }))
Verify Level.width >= 115
Verify spawn point, exit, boss data, and enemies exist
criteria.threshold: Stage 3-1 loads with width >= 115 tiles, has spawn, exit, boss arena, and enemies

criteria.description: Stage 3-2 (Crystal Caverns) has a playable level layout at least 130 tiles wide featuring ice block sliding puzzle sections. Interior cavern aesthetics with crystal formations. Contains mixed enemy types.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, select stage 3-2, enter it

page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, height: Level.height, spawnX: Level.spawnX, spawnY: Level.spawnY, hasExit: typeof Level.exitX === 'number', hasBoss: Level.bossData !== null && Level.bossData !== undefined, enemyCount: Enemies.enemies.length }))
Verify Level.width >= 130
Verify spawn point, exit, boss data, and enemies exist
criteria.threshold: Stage 3-2 loads with width >= 130 tiles, has spawn, exit, boss arena, and enemies

criteria.description: Stage 3-3 (Avalanche Peak) has a playable level layout at least 160 tiles wide featuring auto-scrolling sections. Mountainous terrain with ice platforms. Contains all 3 tundra enemy types.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, select stage 3-3, enter it

page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, height: Level.height, spawnX: Level.spawnX, spawnY: Level.spawnY, hasExit: typeof Level.exitX === 'number', hasBoss: Level.bossData !== null && Level.bossData !== undefined, enemyCount: Enemies.enemies.length }))
Verify Level.width >= 160
Verify spawn point, exit, boss data, and enemies exist
criteria.threshold: Stage 3-3 loads with width >= 160 tiles, has spawn, exit, boss arena, and enemies

criteria.description: Frost Bear boss (stage 3-1) requires exactly 7 hits to defeat. Has a frost beam attack that fires a horizontal beam across the arena. Boss health bar shows in HUD with 'Frost Bear' label and maxHP of 7.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, trigger boss fight by moving player to boss arena
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { type: boss.type, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth, name: boss.name || boss.type }; })
Verify boss type is frost_bear (or equivalent), maxHealth === 7
Verify HUD shows boss name 'Frost Bear' and health bar
criteria.threshold: Frost Bear boss has 7 HP, shows health bar with correct name, has frost beam attack

criteria.description: Crystal Witch boss (stage 3-2) has a crystal shield mechanic requiring 25 total hits to defeat (20 hits to break the crystal shield + 5 direct hits on the witch). The shield visually surrounds the boss and must be destroyed before the witch can be damaged. Boss health bar shows 'Crystal Witch' and maxHP of 25.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-2, trigger boss fight

page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { type: boss.type, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth, name: boss.name || boss.type, hasShield: typeof boss.shieldHP !== 'undefined' || typeof boss.shield !== 'undefined' || typeof boss.shieldHealth !== 'undefined', shieldHP: boss.shieldHP || boss.shieldHealth || null }; })
Verify boss maxHealth === 25 (or shieldHP === 20 and health === 5)
Verify boss has crystal shield mechanic (shield property exists)
criteria.threshold: Crystal Witch has 25 total HP (20 shield + 5 witch), shows health bar with correct name

criteria.description: Yeti Monarch boss (stage 3-3) requires exactly 9 hits to defeat. Has a tiered arena (platforms at different heights) and a boulder throw attack. Boss health bar shows 'Yeti Monarch' and maxHP of 9.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-3, trigger boss fight
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { type: boss.type, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth, name: boss.name || boss.type }; })
Verify boss maxHealth === 9
Verify boss has boulder throw pattern (patterns includes 'boulder' or 'throw' or boss has boulderTimer property)
criteria.threshold: Yeti Monarch has 9 HP, shows health bar with correct name, has boulder throw attack

criteria.description: Boss vulnerability windows work correctly for all tundra bosses. When boss.vulnerable === false, attacks do not reduce boss health. When boss.vulnerable === true, attacks reduce health by 1. This follows the same framework pattern from Sprints 5 and 6.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, trigger Frost Bear boss fight

Test invulnerable: page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; boss.vulnerable = false; const hpBefore = boss.health; Player.x = boss.x - 30; Player.y = boss.y; Player.facing = 1; Player.state = 'attack'; Player.attackTimer = 5; Enemies.checkPlayerAttackCollisions(); return { hpBefore, hpAfter: boss.health }; })
Verify hpBefore === hpAfter

Test vulnerable: page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; boss.vulnerable = true; const hpBefore = boss.health; Player.x = boss.x - 30; Player.y = boss.y; Player.facing = 1; Player.state = 'attack'; Player.attackTimer = 5; Enemies.checkPlayerAttackCollisions(); return { hpBefore, hpAfter: boss.health }; })
Verify hpAfter < hpBefore
criteria.threshold: Tundra bosses follow vulnerability window pattern (no damage when invulnerable, 1 damage when vulnerable)

criteria.description: Tundra bosses have phase transitions. After reducing boss health below 50%, the boss enters phase 2 with modified attack patterns (faster, additional attacks, or new mechanics).
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, trigger Frost Bear boss fight
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { phase: boss.phase, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth }; })
Verify initial phase is 1

page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; boss.health = Math.floor(boss.maxHealth * 0.4); for (let i = 0; i < 60; i++) { if (typeof Enemies.updateBoss === 'function') Enemies.updateBoss(1/60); else if (typeof Enemies.update === 'function') Enemies.update(1/60); } return { phase: boss.phase, health: boss.health }; })
Verify boss.phase has increased (phase > 1) after health dropped below 50% and update ticks ran
criteria.threshold: Boss phase increases when health drops below 50%

criteria.description: Defeating a tundra boss triggers stage completion. Boss defeat sets health to 0, plays defeat animation, and transitions game state to STAGE_COMPLETE.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, trigger Frost Bear boss fight

page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; boss.vulnerable = true; boss.health = 1; Player.x = boss.x - 30; Player.y = boss.y; Player.facing = 1; Player.state = 'attack'; Player.attackTimer = 5; Enemies.checkPlayerAttackCollisions(); return { health: boss.health, state: boss.state }; })
Verify boss health === 0 or boss state is 'dying'/'dead'
Wait for defeat animation, verify GameState transitions to STAGE_COMPLETE
criteria.threshold: Defeating tundra bosses triggers STAGE_COMPLETE state transition

criteria.description: Each tundra stage has coins placed matching WorldMap.STAGES definitions: stage 3-1 has 20 coins, stage 3-2 has 24 coins, stage 3-3 has 30 coins. At least 1 health pickup per stage.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, page.evaluate(() => ({ coins: Collectibles.coins ? Collectibles.coins.length : Level.coinPositions.length, health: Collectibles.healthPickups ? Collectibles.healthPickups.length : Level.healthPositions.length }))
Verify coins === 20 and health >= 1
Enter stage 3-2, verify coins === 24 and health >= 1
Enter stage 3-3, verify coins === 30 and health >= 1
criteria.threshold: Coin totals match STAGES definitions (20, 24, 30); each stage has health pickups

criteria.description: Each tundra stage has at least 3 enemies properly placed within the level geometry — positioned on or near appropriate surfaces. Not floating in empty space. Mix of tundra enemy types used.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, page.evaluate(() => Enemies.enemies.length)
Verify >= 3 enemies
Enter stage 3-2, verify >= 3 enemies
Enter stage 3-3, verify >= 3 enemies
Spot-check that enemy positions correspond to tile geometry
criteria.threshold: Each tundra stage has at least 3 enemies properly positioned in the level

criteria.description: World 3 stages are accessible only after completing all 3 World 2 stages. The world map correctly enforces linear progression — stage 3-1 unlocks after completing stage 2-3.
testSteps: Clear save data, navigate to world map
Verify World 3 nodes are locked (stage 3-1 unlocked === false)
Set save data with stages 1-1 through 2-3 completed
Refresh world map, verify stage 3-1 is now unlocked
criteria.threshold: World 3 locked until World 2 complete; stage 3-1 unlocks after stage 2-3 completion

criteria.description: Selecting a World 3 stage on the world map and pressing Enter loads the correct stage. Each of the 3 tundra stages (3-1, 3-2, 3-3) loads a different, unique level layout — not the Forest, Desert, or test levels. Verified by comparing level name/ID or tile data hash rather than width alone.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, unlock World 3 stages (complete Worlds 1-2 via save data)
Select stage 3-1, press Enter, page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, name: Level.name || Level.id || null, firstRow: Level.tiles ? Level.tiles[0].slice(0,10).join(',') : null }))
Return to world map, select stage 3-2, record same data, verify name or tile data differs from 3-1
Return to world map, select stage 3-3, record same data, verify differs from 3-1 and 3-2
Verify all three tundra stages have distinct level names/IDs or distinct tile data from each other and from World 1/2 stages
criteria.threshold: Three distinct tundra levels load from world map; none reuses Forest or Desert layouts

criteria.description: Boss fights trigger when player reaches the boss arena area of each tundra stage. Camera locks to arena, boss spawns with correct type and health, HUD boss health bar activates with correct name.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1, move player past bossArenaX

page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; return { bossExists: boss !== null && boss !== undefined, bossType: boss ? boss.type : null, bossHP: boss ? boss.maxHealth : null, hudActive: typeof HUD !== 'undefined' ? HUD.bossActive : null }; })
Verify boss spawns with correct type and HP (7 for Frost Bear)
Verify HUD boss bar is active
criteria.threshold: Boss fight triggers in tundra stages; camera locks; boss spawns with health bar

criteria.description: Completing a tundra stage triggers STAGE_COMPLETE state, and returning to world map shows the stage as completed with next stage unlocked. Save system records tundra stage completions, best times, and coin counts.
testSteps: Set save data to have Worlds 1-2 completed and stage 3-1 unlocked
Enter stage 3-1, trigger completion (defeat boss)
Verify GameState transitions to STAGE_COMPLETE
Return to world map, verify stage 3-1 shows completed and stage 3-2 unlocked
page.evaluate(() => { const save = SaveSystem.load(); return { completed: save.completedStages, hasBestTime: '3-1' in (save.bestTimes || {}), hasCoins: '3-1' in (save.coinRecords || {}) }; })
Verify save data includes '3-1' completion
criteria.threshold: Tundra stage completion updates world map, unlocks next stage, saves progress

criteria.description: Fire-lure ice melt puzzle mechanic exists in at least one tundra stage. A fire element (torch, flame source, fire projectile, or lure-able fire enemy) can melt ice blocks to open blocked paths. The player must manipulate fire to melt specific ice obstacles to progress.
testSteps: Enter stage 3-1 or 3-2 (whichever contains fire-lure puzzle)

page.evaluate(() => { const hasFireSource = typeof Level.fireSources !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.torches !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.fireLure !== 'undefined'; const hasMeltableIce = typeof Level.meltableBlocks !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.iceWalls !== 'undefined'; const hasPuzzleElements = typeof Level.puzzleElements !== 'undefined' ? Level.puzzleElements.filter(p => p.type === 'fire' || p.type === 'torch' || p.type === 'fire_lure' || p.type === 'meltable_ice').length : 0; return { hasFireSource, hasMeltableIce, puzzleElementCount: hasPuzzleElements }; })
Verify fire source and meltable ice elements exist (at least one fire source and one meltable ice block)

page.evaluate(() => { const meltable = Level.meltableBlocks || Level.iceWalls || (Level.puzzleElements ? Level.puzzleElements.filter(p => p.type === 'meltable_ice') : []); if (!meltable || meltable.length === 0) return { hasMeltMechanic: false }; const block = Array.isArray(meltable) ? meltable[0] : meltable; return { hasMeltMechanic: true, canMelt: typeof block.melt === 'function' || typeof block.melted !== 'undefined' || typeof block.health !== 'undefined' }; })
Verify meltable ice blocks have melt capability (melt function or melted state property)
criteria.threshold: Fire-lure ice melt puzzle exists with fire sources and meltable ice blocks in a tundra stage

criteria.description: All 3 World 1 Forest stages (1-1, 1-2, 1-3) still load correctly with their original layouts, enemies, and bosses. Forest tile palette and parallax are still rendered. No Forest content has been broken by Tundra additions.
testSteps: Enter stage 1-1, verify Level.width >= 80 and enemies exist
Enter stage 1-2, verify Level.width >= 120 and enemies exist
Enter stage 1-3, verify Level.width >= 150 and enemies exist
Verify Forest enemy types (shroomba, thornvine, barkbeetle) still spawn in Forest stages
Verify Forest bosses still have correct HP (5, 6, 7)
criteria.threshold: All Forest stages load correctly with original content intact

criteria.description: All 3 World 2 Desert stages (2-1, 2-2, 2-3) still load correctly with their original layouts, enemies, bosses, and unique mechanics (quicksand, dark rooms, water). No Desert content has been broken by Tundra additions.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, verify Level.width >= 100 and enemies exist
Enter stage 2-2, verify Level.width >= 130 and enemies exist
Enter stage 2-3, verify Level.width >= 140 and enemies exist
Verify Desert enemy types (sand_skitter, dust_devil, mummy) still spawn
Verify Desert bosses still have correct HP (6, 8, 12)
criteria.threshold: All Desert stages load correctly with original content intact

criteria.description: Player movement vocabulary is fully intact: walk, run, jump (variable height), wall slide, wall jump, coyote time, jump buffering, crouch, melee attack, charge attack, jump attack all function in tundra stages.
testSteps: Enter a tundra stage
Verify player can walk (press right, vx > 0)
Verify player can jump (press Z, vy < 0)
Verify player attack works (press X, state becomes 'attack')
Verify coyote time constant exists (COYOTE_FRAMES === 6)
Verify wall slide works near walls
criteria.threshold: All player movement and combat mechanics function correctly in tundra stages

criteria.description: Game state machine transitions all function: TITLE -> WORLD_MAP -> STAGE -> PAUSED -> STAGE_COMPLETE. HUD renders health hearts, coin counter, boss health bar, and stage name correctly during tundra stages.
testSteps: Verify game starts in TITLE state
Press Enter, verify transition to WORLD_MAP
Select a tundra stage, enter it, verify STAGE state
Press Escape, verify PAUSED state
Resume, verify STAGE state restored
Verify HUD shows hearts and coin counter during STAGE
criteria.threshold: All state transitions work; HUD renders correctly in tundra context

criteria.description: Save system correctly persists across World 1, World 2, and World 3. Completing stages from all three worlds produces a save with all completions, coin records, and best times intact. No data loss across worlds.
testSteps: Set save data with World 1 and World 2 stages completed plus coin/time records
Complete stage 3-1 (via debug/programmatic completion)
page.evaluate(() => SaveSystem.load())
Verify completedStages includes '1-1' through '2-3' AND '3-1'
Verify bestTimes and coinRecords have entries for all three worlds
criteria.threshold: Save data correctly contains World 1, World 2, and World 3 completions without data loss

outOfScope: World 4 Molten Volcano content (stages, enemies, bosses, lava mechanics)
Visual effects polish like screen shake and slow-motion (Sprint 11)
Power Stars collectible (1 per world, hidden)
Magma Slime, Fire Bat, Obsidian Knight enemies (World 4)
notes: Sprint 7 adds World 3: Frozen Tundra, building on the enemy/boss/puzzle frameworks established in Sprints 5-6. Key new mechanics include ice physics (reduced friction on ice tiles/surfaces), ice block sliding puzzles (pushable blocks on frictionless surfaces), auto-scrolling camera (stage 3-3 Avalanche Peak), and fire-lure ice melt puzzles (fire sources that melt ice obstacles to open paths). Three new enemy types extend the Enemies system: Frost Imp (snowball arc projectiles that freeze platforms), Ice Golem (slides on ice, shatters on death), Snow Owl (figure-8 flight, swoops). Three new bosses follow the existing boss framework: Frost Bear (7 HP, frost beam), Crystal Witch (25 HP total = 20 shield + 5 witch, crystal shield mechanic), Yeti Monarch (9 HP, tiered arena, boulder throw). The renderer must support tundra tile drawing, aurora borealis parallax, and falling snow particles. New tile types needed for ice surfaces. Coin counts match WorldMap.STAGES definitions (20, 24, 30). The auto-scroll mechanic in stage 3-3 is a significant new camera mode that forces the player forward and damages/kills the player if they fall behind. All 31 criteria are testable via Playwright page.evaluate() or screenshots. REVISION NOTES: Added C-26 for fire-lure ice melt puzzle (was missing from original proposal). Updated C-06 to test Frost Imp platform-freezing behavior. Updated C-07 to use page.evaluate for programmatic golem kill instead of untestable manual kill. Updated C-09 to add left-edge death/damage test step. Updated C-11 minimum width from 110 to 115 to avoid overlap with World 2 stage 2-1. Updated C-18 to run explicit update ticks via page.evaluate after setting boss health. Updated C-10 to check for Level.forkSections/pathForks arrays before falling back to tile analysis heuristic. Updated C-23 to compare level name/ID and tile data hash instead of width-only comparison.
